title: Add integration test for Beads/Taskulus delete flow
creator: Ryan Alyn Porter

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Added new delete integration scenarios covering create/list/delete across Beads and Taskulus.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: CI still failing at Python coverage gate (99.96%). Added coverage_gate logging to print uncovered files in CI to identify the exact missing line.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: CI uncovered src/taskulus/issue_line.py (98.75%). Added NO_COLOR list formatting scenario and steps in both Python and Rust to cover the no-color path.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Investigating GitHub Actions release workflow failing with 'workflow file issue'. CI itself is green; release.yml fails immediately (no jobs). Suspect workflow_run config; planning to adjust trigger to remove branches filter and gate on head_branch in job if.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Adjusted release workflow trigger to remove workflow_run branches filter and gate on head_branch in publish job. Next: commit, push, verify release workflow no longer fails immediately.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Found YAML parse error in release workflow: embedded Python block not indented; closing PY marker also unindented. Fixed indentation. Local YAML parse now succeeds.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Release workflow rust-crate failed; regex used literal \s so version line not matched. Updated pattern to use \s in raw string so Cargo.toml version can be rewritten.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Release rust-crate failed at clippy: lock file out of date after version bump. Added workflow step to run cargo generate-lockfile after updating Cargo.toml version.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: CI failures: console UI tests crashed due to __dirname in ESM; rust parse errors from missing braces in models.rs and configuration_steps.rs; python black formatting on configuration_steps.py. Applied fixes (ESM-safe path, added missing braces, ran cargo fmt, ran black).

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: CI logs: Rust failed for unused import in cli.rs and missing beads_compatibility in migration ProjectConfiguration; console UI tests timing out on page.goto networkidle. Fixed by removing unused import, adding beads_compatibility=false, and switching Playwright goto to domcontentloaded with 60s timeout. Also updated behave tags to exclude @console.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: CI run shows TagExpressionError in behave tags and missing beads_compatibility in rust workflow_steps. Updated behave tags to 'not wip and not console', added beads_compatibility=false in workflow_steps initializer, and adjusted console UI steps to wait for settings button instead of missing header text.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: New requirement: add full interoperability checks using the real Beads CLI + real Beads repo data. CI must install Beads CLI, build Taskulus Python + Rust, then run a final integration stage after the normal CI gates. Plan to add a dedicated integration script and a new CI job that runs after existing jobs and validates create/list/update/delete across Beads CLI, Taskulus Python, and the Rust binary using a cloned Beads repo.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Added tools/run_beads_interop_suite.py to run full Beads CLI interoperability checks with real Beads repo data. CI now includes a beads-interop job that runs after python, rust, console-ui, performance, and python-install; it builds the Rust release binary, builds the Beads CLI from the cloned repo, and runs the interoperability suite.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Ran tools/run_beads_interop_suite.py. It failed building Beads CLI due to missing ICU headers (go-icu-regex: unicode/regex.h not found). Added apt-get install pkg-config libicu-dev to the beads-interop CI job to satisfy Beads build dependencies.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Ran tools/run_beads_interop_suite.py locally using the installed bd binary. It fails at Taskulus Python show with 'multiple parents'. load_beads_issues converts all Beads records, so any Beads issue with multiple parent-child dependencies causes the entire read to fail. The real Beads repo contains such records (e.g., bd-98c4e1fa.1). We need to decide how Taskulus should handle multiple parents for Beads interop.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: New request: create sample/fixture project data for more extensive testing. Need new feature to manage AGENTS.md: ensure a Taskulus section exists near top; find a header containing 'Taskulus' or insert new section after H1 as first H2. Also decide on CLI command name and behavior.

custom.beads_acceptance_criteria: Scenario covers create -> list in Taskulus and Beads -> delete -> verify removal in both.
custom.beads_notes: Add Gherkin ensuring issue appears in both list outputs after creation and absent after deletion.
